message:
docs: align conformance claim to actual 430+ packets / 1249 fixtures (br-frankenpandas-zgqj)

Reality-check MEDIUM zgqj was filed 24h ago when bootstrap_cases
sat at 1212/1249 with 37 fixture mismatches blocking 8 top-level
aggregate gates (ci_gate_g6, ci_pipeline, e2e_suite, differential_*,
packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases, failure_forensics,
grouped_reports, differential_all_packets_green). Two signals were
off:

  (a) UNDERCLAIM — README capability table said "20+ packet suites"
      but a count of fixtures/packets/ directory shows 430 unique
      packet IDs (fp_p2c_001 ... fp_p2c_43x) across 1249 JSON fixture
      files — a ~20x understatement.

  (b) OVERCLAIM for current health — 37 fixture mismatches made the
      "Yes" cell green-for-wrong-reason until the burn-down landed.

Both have now resolved in-tree. Re-ran under rch:

    cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib
    → 387 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

    cargo test -p fp-conformance (all targets, lib + integration)
    → every suite green; 13 ignored (unrelated long-path flags);
      zero failures anywhere

packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases AND
differential_all_packets_green both pass — meaning every fixture
in every packet clears the differential gate against the pandas
oracle.

Two surgical README edits to make both the scale and the health
claim match reality:

1. Capability table (README.md:37) — the "FrankenPandas" cell for
   the conformance row was "Yes (20+ packet suites)" and is now
   "Yes (430+ packet suites, 1249 fixtures, all green)". Captures
   scale and current health in one cell.

2. Conformance regen section (README.md:856) — the prose that said
   "20+ packet suites covering alignment, join, groupby, concat,
   filter, CSV, dtype, null semantics, and more" is now "430+
   packet suites spanning 1249 fixtures cover alignment, join,
   groupby, concat, filter, CSV, dtype, null semantics, resample,
   rolling, groupby rolling/resample, datetime accessors, string
   accessors, and more — all currently green under `cargo test -p
   fp-conformance`". The expanded coverage list reflects what has
   actually landed in the packet corpus since the README was last
   touched (the groupby rolling/resample families are br-fmmq /
   br-28y8 / br-339q / br-icm3 / br-i2j8, all closed today).

No follow-up epic; the fixture burn-down is done. The "track
per-failing-fixture beads" path the original bead suggested was
the right call in retrospect — it appears to have happened via
parallel-agent tick activity (multiple small commits against
case_id patterns) rather than a single cc/cod-led sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -34,7 +34,7 @@
 | Row MultiIndex | Yes | No | Scaffolded (standalone type; DataFrame row-axis integration pending — see Limitations) |
 | Categorical dtype | Yes | Yes | Yes (metadata layer) |
 | 7 IO formats (CSV/JSON/JSONL/Parquet/Excel/SQL/Feather) | Yes (SQL: any SQLAlchemy engine) | Partial | Yes (SQL: SQLite-only via rusqlite; PostgreSQL/MySQL planned) |
-| Conformance testing against pandas oracle | - | - | Yes (20+ packet suites) |
+| Conformance testing against pandas oracle | - | - | Yes (430+ packet suites, 1249 fixtures, all green) |
 
 ## Quick Example
 
@@ -853,7 +853,7 @@ let merged = df1.merge_with_options(&df2, &["key"], JoinType::Inner,
 ./scripts/phase2c_gate_check.sh
 ```
 
-Regenerates conformance packet artifacts and fails closed if any parity report or gate is not green. 20+ packet suites covering alignment, join, groupby, concat, filter, CSV, dtype, null semantics, and more.
+Regenerates conformance packet artifacts and fails closed if any parity report or gate is not green. 430+ packet suites spanning 1249 fixtures cover alignment, join, groupby, concat, filter, CSV, dtype, null semantics, resample, rolling, groupby rolling/resample, datetime accessors, string accessors, and more — all currently green under `cargo test -p fp-conformance`.
 
 ## Missing Data Handling
